description:
You are an artifact-manager who needs to curate a small binary repository by
    mirroring one (and only one) file from an upstream share into your local
    collection.  Finish the job with a single terminal command and keep a log of
    what happened.

    1. Upstream “remote” share  
       • Path:  /home/user/remote_repo/libalpha/libalpha-1.2.3.tar.gz  

    2. Local curated repository (must exist when you are done)  
       • Directory:  /home/user/artifacts/libalpha/  
       • Expected file after the sync:  /home/user/artifacts/libalpha/libalpha-1.2.3.tar.gz  

    3. Log requirements  
       • Create directory  /home/user/sync_logs/  (if it does not yet exist).  
       • Capture every byte of stdout produced by the sync command and save it
         unmodified to the text file  
         /home/user/sync_logs/libalpha_sync.log  
       • The automated tester will check that the log has:  
         a) The very first non-blank line starting with the exact word  
            “sending” (all lowercase).  
         b) At least one line that contains the literal substring  
            “libalpha-1.2.3.tar.gz”.  
         c) One of the last three non-blank lines beginning with the word  
            “sent” and another beginning with “received” (rsync’s transfer
            summary).  They may appear on the same line or on two consecutive
            lines—either is accepted.

    4. Success criteria checked by the grader  
       • /home/user/artifacts/libalpha/libalpha-1.2.3.tar.gz exists.  
       • That file’s size is identical to the upstream file.  
       • /home/user/sync_logs/libalpha_sync.log exists and respects all three
         bullet points in section 3.  

    5. Constraints  
       • Achieve the transfer with one single terminal command  
         (rsync is recommended but not strictly required if the above criteria
         are met).  
       • Do not alter the upstream file or path.  
       • Do not hard-link or move the original file; the curated repository must
         contain its own copy.  

    Execute your command, verify that the two files now exist in the correct
    places, and make sure the log file meets the exact formatting requirements
    so the automated tests can validate your work.
truth:
<!--
        Pre-existing upstream file the agent must copy
    -->
    <upstream-file path="/home/user/remote_repo/libalpha/libalpha-1.2.3.tar.gz">
This is libalpha binary version 1.2.3
    </upstream-file>

    <!--
        What the grader will look for **after** the agent runs
    -->
    <expected-local-file path="/home/user/artifacts/libalpha/libalpha-1.2.3.tar.gz">
This is libalpha binary version 1.2.3
    </expected-local-file>

    <logfile path="/home/user/sync_logs/libalpha_sync.log">
        • first_non_blank_line_starts_with = "sending"
        • must_contain_substring            = "libalpha-1.2.3.tar.gz"
        • one_of_last_three_lines_starts_with = "sent"
        • one_of_last_three_lines_starts_with = "received"
    </logfile>
name: task_000000_38e76ef1
